name: desk
description: a quiet room of work for long-running agents — the universal substrate underneath whichever overlay (corporate worker, autonomous agent, personal coding agent) sits on top. drawers for tracks, manilla envelopes for tasks, a corkboard for friction notes, a small reference shelf for lessons earned; archive at the back of the room, still browsable. provides the workspace conventions, lifecycle states, dispatch, friction registers, and engineering posture skills that turn an agent from one-off helper into a long-running, accountable, learning-over-time worker. cross-context portable: the same skills serve enterprise worker overlays, autonomous agents, and personal coding agents — each consumer agent declares its own workspace path via a $DESK placeholder binding in its preamble, so the layout and ceremonies travel without depending on whose desk it is. depends on work-suite for the doing-phase skills (ideator / planner / doer / merger).

agentView.evidence: Claude Code 2.1.161 exposes `--agent` on sessions and `claude agents --agent` for Agent View dispatched sessions; Agent View is documented as background sessions running full Claude Code conversations.

backgroundSessionInheritance.evidence: Claude Code 2.1.161 `claude agents --help` exposes `--agent` and plugin loading for dispatched sessions; use Agent View for background worker sessions.
backgroundSessionInheritance.fallback: Launch foreground with `claude --agent desk:worker` when Agent View is unavailable.
